comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the features of Ichinichi, particularly its design choices regarding time management and end-to-end encryption. Users express curiosity about how the app handles daily summaries and encryption processes.

comments.author: redgridtactical
comments.text: The read-only past is a really smart design choice. I build local-first apps and it's always tempting to add edit-everything...
comments.analysis: This comment provides a well-reasoned perspective on the app's design choices and raises a relevant technical question.

comments.author: jcynix
comments.text: Nice, and I like the idea that the past is fixed, but ... is there a way to define the point of rollover to the next day? My 'days'...
comments.analysis: The comment expresses a personal concern about the app's functionality, contributing to the discussion with a practical question.

comments.author: elxr
comments.text: How does the E2E work in terms of user flow? I assume a you need a password? Do you need to enter the password every time...
comments.analysis: This comment raises important questions about user experience but lacks depth in exploring the implications of E2E encryption.
